3 Valverde scored a stunning 22-minute hat-trick in the first half against Man City Credit: Getty

Man City's start to the first leg of their last 16 tie in Madrid couldn't have started much worse, with Valverde putting them to the world spectacularly.

He scored his opening goal after 20 minutes, rounding City goalkeeper Gianluigi Donnarumma to tap into an empty net.

Then, just seven minutes later, he superbly fired into the bottom corner past Donnarumma again to make it 2-0.

And Valverde saved the best until last, netting an incredible third goal in just 22 minutes to score his first career hat-trick.

The 27-year-old raced into the box and received a neat chipped pass from Brahim Diaz, meeting it with a perfect touch.

His first touch lifted the ball perfectly over Marc Guehi's head, before he ran onto it again and volleyed home before it touched the ground.

On the sumptuous hat-trick goal, talkSPORT's Scott Minto said on commentary: "That is a world-class goal from a world-class player.

"Anyone who gets the chance to see this goal at any point in the future, please do... he's running at pace, the first touch is perfect, over Guehi and enough for him to volley it in.

"It's just an unbelievable goal... it's three moments of genius from the same player."

Host Adrian Durham then later added: "It's a Puskas contender."

3 Valverde's third goal of the game was labelled as a 'Puskas contender' Credit: Getty

Valverde's treble was all the match had to offer in terms of goals with the Bernabeu clash finishing 3-0, although the home side missed a glorious chance to get their fourth in the second half when Vinicius Jr had a penalty saved by Gianluigi Donnarumma.

On top of his potentially Puskas-winning strike, Valverde also joined a footballing icon with his first-half hat-trick.

He is just the second player to score a first-half hat-trick against an English opponent in the Champions League after Messi did so for Barcelona against Arsenal in April 2010.

Bellingham can't believe it

Not only were talkSPORT's commentary team in awe of Valverde's third goal, but his injured teammate Jude Bellingham was too.

Bellingham, who is sidelined due to a hamstring injury picked up not long after undergoing shoulder surgery, was left with his mouth wide open as he watched a replay.

His eyes widened and he was left in disbelief as he clapped into the air whilst watching on before making a praise gesture.

3 Bellingham was left in complete awe of his teammate's incredible goal

Man City slammed

After Valverde's hat-trick, Durham then went on a half-time rant about Man City's performance that saw them go 3-0 down.

He brutally summarised on-air: "Marc Guehi, ordinary, he just stands out of the way for that third goal.

"Antoine Semenyo, out of his depth, has offered nothing.

"O'Reilly's doing a Kinsky, Doku and Savinho, we highlighted it before, they are no threat whatsoever...Man City have been truly terrible."

Pep Guardiola's side went on to lose the first leg 3-0 and they have a mountain to climb in the second leg back in Manchester, which will be live on talkSPORT next Tuesday.

Football.title: Former Arsenal player joins Will Ferrell and Magic Johnson as part-owner of MLS club LAFC
Football.content:
Former Arsenal player joins Will Ferrell and Magic Johnson as part-owner of MLS club LAFC

Los Angeles FC has added some extra star power to its vast ownership group.

In a statement, the Major League Soccer (MLS) club announced Carlos Vela has made the transition from former player to owner.

3 Vela's long-running connection to Los Angeles FC will continue

“LAFC has always been more than a club to me — it is home,” Vela said.

“From the first day, we set out to build something special for Los Angeles, and I am incredibly proud of what we have accomplished together.

"Becoming an owner is an honor and a responsibility I take seriously.

"I’m excited to continue supporting the club, our players, and our fans as we keep building for the future.”

Who else is part of LAFC's ownership group?

Vela now sits alongside Hollywood icon Will Ferrell and NBA legend Earl 'Magic' Johnson as one of the many individuals who are part of LAFC's ownership group.

So too is Italian great Giorgio Chiellini, who finished his playing career with LAFC in December 2023.

LAFC also confirmed Vela's appointment to their ownership group would not take away from his role as the club's first Black and Gold Ambassador, which entails representing the California-based club at global events along with strengthening ties with local supporters.

Vela's inclusion continues his long-standing ties to the club, which he joined 2017.

In fact, the former Mexico international was LAFC's first ever player having signed on as a Designated Player one year before the club's inaugural campaign.

A glimpse at Vela's staggering MLS stats

Vela enjoyed six seasons with the club and inspired it to five trophies, including the MLS Cup in 2022.

3 Ferrell became a part-owner of LAFC in January 2016 Credit: Getty

When he called time on his playing career last May, Vela finished as not only a club legend but an all-time MLS icon.

Vela pulled on a LAFC shirt on 152 occasions, scoring 78 goals and assisting 42, all of which are club-record figures.

The 37-year-old's best campaign for the Californian outfit came in 2019, when he slammed home a whopping 34 goals and chalked up ten assists from 31 regular season games.

Vela's 34-goal haul remains an league record for the most goals scored by a player in a single campaign.

Unsurprisingly, Vela's staggering 2019 season earned him MLS MVP honours.

Where else did Vela play in his career?

Vela's stint in California was the dream way to bookend a career that included stops at Arsenal, West Bromwich Albion and Real Sociedad.

3 Vela made just 29 Premier League appearances for the Gunners across three seasons Credit: AFP

The Mexican was on Arsenal's books from November 2005 but did not make his senior debut for the club until August 2008.

By the time he departed north London for Real Sociedad in July 2012, Vela had made a total of 64 appearances and scored 11 goals across all competitions for the Gunners.

Vela would remain in San Sebastian until December 2017, when he moved to Los Angeles.

Football.title: Igor Tudor faces Tottenham player revolt as replacements, including club legend, eyed for job
Football.content:
Igor Tudor has already alienated many of the Tottenham Hotspur players, talkSPORT understands.

Tudor is set to remain in charge in the short term but it appears unlikely he will last beyond this month's international break.

3 Tudor's time in north London is not going to plan Credit: Getty

His treatment of goalkeeper Antonin Kinsky during Tuesday's hammering at Atletico Madrid did not go down well within the dressing room.



The Spurs hierarchy are understood to have sounded out potential replacements for the Croat, who has had only four games in charge, losing all four.

Who could take over from Tudor?

Possible options include former interim boss and coach Ryan Mason and Spurs legend Robbie Keane, currently in charge of Hungarian side Ferencvaros.

Mason, who has overseen 13 Spurs matches across two previous caretaker stints, is available after he was sacked by West Bromwich Albion in January.

Prior to his appointment by the Baggies last July, Mason had been a part of Tottenham's first team coaching set-up since 2019.



Another name heavily linked with a return to north London is Mauricio Pochettino.

However, he is committed to the United States men's national team for the upcoming World Cup, meaning he would only be a viable option at the end of the season.



Crucially, talkSPORT understands Pochettino remains open to the possibility of a second spell in charge of Tottenham.

The Argentine is also in no doubt about how some fans would feel about a return to Tottenham after a handful of supporters serenaded him on a plane.



How Tudor's reign has unravelled

Tudor was appointed in February following Thomas Frank's sacking, with the Dane lasting just eight months into a three-year deal.

3 Tudor has Tottenham in an uncontrollable nosedive Credit: talkSPORT

Despite arriving with a reputation of being a firefighter, Tudor has only poured fuel on the flames.

Under the Croatian, Tottenham have slid perilously closer to the relegation zone.

Ahead of their visit to Liverpool on Sunday, Tottenham lie 16th in the Premier League with 29 points, only one more than 18th-placed West Ham United.



Adding further to Tottenham's woes is the fact they have not won a league fixture since December 28.

But the drought is one that must be snapped sooner than later given there are just nine league fixtures remaining this term.

Unfortunately for Tottenham, securing safety will be no easy task given they are without star defender Micky Van de Ven for Sunday's trip to Anfield.

3 Van de Ven's red card against Palace was the first time he'd been sent off in his club career Credit: Getty

The Netherlands international is suspended having picked up a red card during Tottenham's last league outing, which was a 3-1 loss at home to Palace.



Van de Ven is under no illusions about the difficulty of what lies ahead and gave an insight into the glum mood that has engulfed the club following Tottenham's loss to Atleti on Tuesday.

Speaking to Dutch broadcaster Ziggo Sport, Van de Ven said: "Right now we're just taking blow after blow after blow.

"It's just really tough, and now we also have an important match this weekend that I won't be able to play in because I'm suspended.

"It's a really terrible period, I can tell you that. It's really, really awful."

Football.title: Arsenal set-piece coach has words on touchline with rival after Gunners get set pieced
Football.content:
Arsenal set-piece coach has words on touchline with rival after Gunners get set pieced

Arsenal had a taste of their own medicine on Wednesday evening as Bayer Leverkusen scored from a corner against them.

And Leverkusen boss, Kasper Hjulmand, made sure to instantly find the Gunners' set-piece coach to have a cheeky word.

3 Robert Andrich scored against Arsenal in the one way the Gunners are usually best at Credit: Getty

Mikel Arteta's men are hoping to boost their chances of a quadruple this season in their Champions League last 16 tie.

But they fell behind against their German opposition just after half-time in the first leg at the BayArena.

And the fashion they conceded was a very rare one, as they were beaten by their own game in Europe.

Leverkusen's Robert Andrich arrived unmarked at the back post from a deep, swinging corner to head into the back of the net.

It was Arsenal's first goal conceded from a corner in a Champions League match since November 2024 against Sporting Lisbon.

Following the goal, cameras then showed Leverkusen boss Hjulmand rushing over to speak to the Gunners' set-piece coach Nicolas Jover.

Jover has built a reputation as one of the best in the business, with his side netting over 30 times from set pieces this season.

But after getting the better of his tactics in a rare feat, Hjulmand made sure to go and have a word, with the pair seemingly speaking in good jest as they laughed together.

Andrich's header looked to be proving to be a costly one for Arsenal, with Leverkusen leading 1-0 heading into the final stages.

However, a late Kai Havertz penalty against his old club helped extend their unbeaten streak to 11 games, with the tie ending 1-1.

3 Andrich's header from a corner was the first Arsenal had conceded since 2024 Credit: Getty

Set-piece stats

Bayer Leverkusen clearly came out to stop Arsenal's set-piece threat, even posting on their social media about it.

In a post on X, the German club showed an edited picture of a sign on the pitch at the BayArena, reading: "NO CORNERS ALLOWED!!!"

They wrote 'Worth a try' in the caption and then later, after the goal, commented on it again, saying: "Well, this is awkward…"

Leverkusen ironically scored from a corner themselves, but also only let Arsenal take three corners during the entire game.

The Gunners didn't have any first-half corners at all, which was just the fifth time in 47 matches this season that they haven't had one.

And it was the first time ever that they didn't have one in Europe.

3 A late penalty from Havertz saved Arsenal from a defeat in Germany Credit: AFP

Havertz saves Arsenal

In his first game back in Leverkusen against his former side, Havertz stepped up to score under immense pressure.

The German netted from the spot in the 89th minute, to claim what could be a crucial draw heading into the last 16 second leg.

He now has more Champions League goals against Bayer Leverkusen (one) than he scored for them in eight games (none).

The Gunners will now just have to win at the Emirates Stadium in the second leg next Tuesday to reach the quarter-finals.

talkSPORT will have live commentary of the game next week.

To tune in to talkSPORT or talkSPORT 2 through the website, click HERE for the live stream. Listen via the talkSPORT app, on DAB digital radio, through your smart speaker and on 1089 or 1053 AM.

Football.title: Arsenal have their own Lamine Yamal – he’s a better talent than Bukayo Saka
Football.content:
Arsenal have their own Lamine Yamal – he’s a better talent than Bukayo Saka

Arsenal have a serious gem on their hands in Max Dowman.

The 16-year-old has made six first-team appearances this season, the latest being the Gunners' 2-1 win over Mansfield Town in the FA Cup fifth round.

4 Dowman delivered his best performance for Arsenal yet in the win at Mansfield Credit: Getty

Dowman, who played 77 minutes in attacking midfield, could and perhaps should have got on the scoresheet.

Dowman delights against Mansfield

He pounced on a loose pass from Mansfield goalkeeper Liam Roberts just outside the 18-yard box and drove towards goal before firing off a shot, only to be denied by the latter's outstretched left boot.

Dowman forced Roberts into another superb save early in the second half when he burst past two Mansfield players on the outskirts of the centre circle and rolled the ball past another prior to letting a left-footed effort fly.

Those were just two bright moments in a display that was mature beyond Dowman's teenage years as he stood head and shoulders above his teammates.

Remarkable stats behind Dowman's best showing yet

According to FotMob, Dowman registered the highest expected goals (xG) of all players in the match at 1.01, the second-highest expected assists (xA) with 0.39, the most shots on target (three), the most touches in the opposition box (13), was fouled the most (three times), and won the most ground duels among Arsenal players (seven from 12).

Safe to say, the sky is the limit for Dowman, who even won a penalty on his Premier League debut at age 15.

Aside from remarkable ability to slalom past defenders with the ball at his feet, Gunners legend Perry Groves was quick to point out another valuable trait already on display from the teenager.

And, in a major warning for Arsenal's rivals, it is one that will make Dowman even more of a nightmare to defend going forward.

Groves: Dowman 'better' than Saka at same age

"He's a generational talent, without any shadow of a doubt," Groves said on the latest episode of Inside Gooners.

"If you look at the age groups, with (Bukayo) Saka coming through, (Ethan) Nwaneri coming through, (Myles) Lewis-Skelly coming through, he's a better player at 16 than even what Bukayo Saka was.

4 Saka is the most successful Hale End graduate in some time Credit: Getty

"I remember Steve Bould, I talked to him when he was in charge of the Under-18s about Bukayo Saka and he said to me, 'Make no mistake, I've got one at the minute, he's going to be a superstar.' He was talking about Bukayo Saka.

"The thing with Max Dowman is, we've got a street footballer in Eberechi Eze. He's (Eze) learnt his football in the cages in south London.

"If you look at Max Dowman, he is a street footballer. What I mean by that is, he does things that you can't coach, you can't teach. It is all natural in his body. He doesn't overthink stuff, he just does it.

"I got a bit of stick when I saw him against Manchester United's Under-18s, he ran the show. He was 15, against 18-year-olds. I said he reminded me of his balance and the way he moves like Lionel Messi.

"But the thing that stands out with Max Dowman is, Lionel Messi is five-foot-eight. He's six-foot.

"So what's going to happen is, he's still filling out, he's nowhere near as strong as what he's going to be in his body as he fills out."

4 Dowman withstood several physical challenges from Mansfield Town players Credit: Getty

Inside Gooners host Adrian Clarke went a step further, stating Dowman is on the same trajectory as a heavy Ballon d'Or favourite.

Dowman's Yamal destiny?

"The speed of his dribbling is something I haven't really seen outside of Messi," Clarke said.

"I think he's going to be of Lamine Yamal's level. I think that's what we're talking about here with Max Dowman. He is good enough."

Another standout element of Dowman's game Groves pointed out was the teenager's fearlessness.

Some players at Dowman's age would, perhaps understandably, rein their game in should they misplace a pass or be sent flying by a crunching tackle from a rival defender.

Yet Groves does not see any of that in Dowman, who evidently has the support of Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta to make in-game mistakes and learn on the job.

4 Despite being just 18, Yamal is a serious contender to win the Ballon d'Or this year Credit: AFP

"There's no fear," Groves said.

"So when he goes to take players on or lets it run across his body and he drops his shoulder, if he gets tackled, he doesn't think, 'Well, next time I better lend it.'

"He goes, 'Actually, no, I'm going to do it again.'"

Football.title: EFL manager leaves club after just six games in charge
Football.content:
EFL manager leaves club after just six games in charge

Barrow are on the hunt for a fourth permanent manager this season after Dino Maamria departed the club.

The decision was made less than 24 hours after a 2-0 loss to Bristol Rovers.

3 The Barrow role was Maamria's first in management since December 2023 Credit: Getty

It was the League Two side's fourth defeat from six games under Maamria, who was only appointed on February 11.



The result left Barrow sitting 22nd in the fourth tier with 28 points and outside of the relegation zone only on goal difference.

Barrow's statement on Maamria

"Barrow AFC can confirm Dino Maamria has left the club today," the club said in a statement.

"We would like to thank Dino for his efforts and wish him well for the future.

"Sam Foley, supported by Simon Ireland and David Worrall, will take the team until the end of the season.



"We recognise that this has been a very disappointing season with far too much managerial change. As the Board of Directors, we understand that the buck stops with us. We believe this change gives us the best chance of remaining in the football league."

Barrow added a supporters Q&A session will go ahead as planned on Friday, with members of the board due to attend and answer questions.



With Maamria now gone, Barrow will need a fresh face in the dugout yet again.

Managerial merry-go-round at Barrow

The Bluebirds began the campaign with Andy Whing in charge, but he was axed on December 10.



Paul Gallagher was announced as Whing's successor on January 2.

3 Maamria won his first game in charge but endured a horror run for the rest of his tenure Credit: Getty

However, five defeats in as many games forced Barrow's hand as the 41-year-old departed the club on February 11, with Maamria appointed the same day.

Whoever takes the reins from Maamria will have a tricky task to preserve Barrow's status as a Football League club.

How have Barrow fared since promotion from the National League?

Barrow got promoted to the fourth tier in 2020 having won the National League in the 2019/20 campaign.

Finishes of 21st and 22nd ensued in Barrow's first and second seasons as a League Two club.



But their fortunes were transformed in the 2022/23 season as Pete Wild oversaw a stunning ninth-place finish.

The Bluebirds improved on the placing the following term as they ended it in eighth.

3 Barrow are in danger of becoming a non-league club again Credit: Getty

However, they dropped back down the table last season and ended it in 16th.

Barrow's next game on Saturday sees them host Accrington Stanley, with kick-off scheduled for 3pm.

News.title: My sister died at home after spending £1.5k of alcohol every MONTH on apps… foul delivery drivers sent her love notes
News.content:
A GRIEVING sister is campaigning to ban alcohol sales on delivery apps after her sibling spent up to £1,500 a month on booze brought straight to her door.

Alexandria Hughes, 31, has launched a petition calling for tighter safeguards on apps including Just Eat, Deliveroo and Uber Eats to stop vulnerable people accessing dangerous amounts of alcohol.





Her sister Zoe Hughes, 35, from Lincoln, Lincolnshire, had struggled with alcoholism for several years while dealing with personal issues.

But after Zoe was found dead at her home in July 2023, Alexandria said she was stunned by what she discovered while sorting through her sister’s affairs.

She claimed bank records showed Zoe had been spending more than £1,000 a month on alcohol ordered through delivery platforms – something the family had no idea about.



Alexandria, an NHS cancer navigator, believes her sister’s battle with alcoholism was worsened by the “easy and unchecked access” to booze through apps.

She also said messages found on Zoe’s phone suggested delivery drivers had allegedly misused contact details taken from orders to send unsolicited texts, gifts and even love notes.

Calling the behaviour a “gross violation of her privacy and dignity”, Alexandria said it showed how vulnerable customers could be exploited.

Devastated Alexandria, also from Lincoln, is now urging the Government to either ban alcohol sales via delivery apps or introduce strict monitoring systems and regulations.



She said Zoe’s drinking had escalated dramatically over several years, explaining that what began as casual drinking eventually spiralled into severe alcoholism despite therapy and rehab attempts.

According to Alexandria, her sister went from being drink-dependent to a full-blown alcoholic within three to four years.

By December 2022, Zoe’s drinking had worsened significantly, and over the following seven months she is said to have spent between £1,000 and £1,500 each month ordering alcohol and cigarettes online.

Tragically, Zoe was later found at the bottom of her stairs on July 24, 2023. Her death was recorded as misadventure.

And Alexandria believes the convenience of delivery apps can become a dangerous tool for those battling addiction.



She argued that pubs are not allowed to serve people who are clearly intoxicated, questioning why delivery apps can continue supplying alcohol without similar checks.

The grieving sister also claimed the issue is made worse by a lack of support for people struggling with addiction, claiming many are unable to access funded rehab places and are left drinking alone at home.

Alexandria suggested apps should allow people to declare they are alcoholics in order to block alcohol advertising and offers – similar to systems used by gambling platforms.



Zoe’s addiction was also linked to wider health struggles.

Alexandria said addictions often go hand-in-hand with mental health conditions, adding that her sister had also battled bulimia and anorexia.

Despite her difficulties, Alexandria described Zoe as a devoted mum to her two children.

She said friends and family always saw Zoe as a kind and loving parent who never forgot birthdays or heartfelt messages, adding that she adored her children and wanted to be happy.



Alexandria now hopes her petition – to either ban alcohol deliveries entirely or introduce strict rules – will raise awareness and push for change.

The Change.org petition has already gathered more than 2,000 signatures, with Alexandria saying dozens of people have contacted her offering support.

She believes the system needs urgent reform, arguing that someone in a pub would not be served multiple bottles of wine if they were clearly intoxicated – and questioning why delivery services are treated differently.

Just Eat, Deliveroo, and Uber Eats were approached for comment.

An Uber Eats spokesperson said: “We are sorry to hear about these tragic events in 2023.



“The safety and wellbeing of our community is our priority and we have an ongoing partnership with Drinkaware to implement further alcohol safety measures, including clear signposting to support resources and the ability to restrict platform access for customers where necessary.

“To help ensure responsible consumption, every alcohol delivery requires the courier to confirm the recipient’s date of birth as per their physical ID and conduct a sobriety check before an order can be completed.”

Where to get help if you think you have a drinking problem If you think you might have a problem with booze then you may need to seek help. This might be the case if you often feel the need to have a drink or if you get into trouble because of your drinking. If other people have warned you about your drink and it’s causing you problems then a good place to start is your GP. There are other places you can go to get help: Drinkline: Call 0300 123 1110 (weekdays 9am to 8pm, weekends 11am to 4pm).

AA – a free self help group that follows the 12 step programme

Al-Anon Family Groups – a group for friends and family members impacted by drinking

We Are With You – for individuals, families and communities struggling – call 0808 8010 750 – if you’re over 50 and worried about booze

Adfam – local support groups and message boards

National Association for Children of Alcoholics (Nacoa) – call 0800 358 3456

SMART Recovery – to help people discover if they have a problem

Uber also stated the events took place three years ago, and they have not been provided with details to investigate the account or potential courier behaviour.

The company claims this remains unconfirmed as taking place on the Uber Eats platform.

A Deliveroo spokesperson said: “The welfare of our customers is of utmost importance to us.



“All retailers selling alcohol on the platform must adhere to the same licensing, marketing and regulatory restrictions to in-store sales, and age verification checks are mandatory.

“Our specialist account suspension or deactivation policy is enforced when we are notified of a customer safety concern.”

A Just Eat spokesperson said: “We take these matters incredibly seriously and have robust processes and policies in play to ensure any alcohol delivered via the platform is done so legally and responsibly.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“Additionally, we can provide extra support by blocking accounts or addresses, and we would urge anyone with specific concerns about a loved-one to get in contact with us via customer service.

“Whilst drivers are independent self-employed contractors we take any reports of misconduct extremely seriously and will ban drivers from working on the platform as is appropriate – we are urgently investigating with the information that has been shared with us.”

News.title: Starmer chose to ignore Mandelson risks – will axed Labour peer’s downfall now bring PM’s whole regime down with him?
News.content:
THESE files reveal Peter Mandelson to be a money-hungry politician more concerned with making a buck than the reputation of his country.

They also raise serious questions about the judgment of Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer.





Why did he make Mandelson US ambassador when his links to convicted paedophile Jeffrey Epstein were well known?

These files leave the PM with nowhere to hide. It is all there in black and white.

He was warned of the risks and he chose to ignore them.

First was the Foreign Office due diligence report in December 2024 warning of the “reputational risk” of Mandelson’s relationship with Epstein.

The Labour peer remained friendly with Epstein after the paedo’s 2008 conviction and even stayed in Epstein’s home while he was in jail, it stated.

Then there were the warnings of Jonathan Powell, the PM’s National Security Adviser.

He was concerned Mandelson’s appointment was “weirdly rushed”, the documents say.

He raised his concerns with the PM’s chief of staff Morgan McSweeney and was told “issues had been addressed”.



What were the issues? And were his concerns properly considered or brushed aside?

The files also reveal Mandelson asked for a £547,000 pay-off after being sacked as US ambassador. In the end he still walked off with £75,000.

He also emailed the Foreign Office for help returning to the UK with the “maximum dignity and minimum media intrusion”, adding: “I remain a crown/civil servant and expect to be treated as such.”

Mandelson was more worried about making money and his own ego than any damage he wreaked on our country — and the Special Relationship.



But the files also show Starmer and his team blindly ignored the many warnings that came their way.

We will learn more about this sorry saga. But one thing is already clear — Mandelson’s political downfall is total.

The question now is whether he ends up bringing Starmer’s whole regime down with him.

News.title: Sir Keir KNEW Mandelson was ‘reputational risk’ over Epstein ties before ‘weirdly rushed’ appointment as US ambassador
News.content:
THE PM knew Peter Mandelson was a “reputational risk” when he made him US ambassador — before firing him with a £75,000 pay-off, damning files reveal.

Sir Keir Starmer’s national security adviser also said the Labour peer’s appointment was “weirdly rushed”.





The PM’s judgment was called into serious question yesterday over his handling of Peter Mandelson’s appointment.

A damning 147-page dossier, released by the Government, showed Sir Keir was warned of the “reputational risk” given the Labour peer’s ties to paedophile Jeffrey Epstein.

National security adviser Jonathan Powell raised concerns to the PM about Mandelson before his December 2024 appointment.

Sir Keir was also told Mandelson had carried on his relationship with Epstein after his 2008 conviction.

Mandelson, in the role from February to September 2025, requested a £547,000 pay-off after his sacking. He ended up with a taxpayer-funded £75,000.

Shadow Minister Neil O’Brien last night claimed that payment was “hush money” to stop him going public at any future employment tribunal.

The files also showed Sir Keir was warned about Lord Mandelson’s links to China and Russia.



And it emerged the peer also wanted to work with Reform UK leader Nigel Farage “as a bridgehead” to the White House.

Sir Keir has previously said that Mandelson “lied repeatedly” to Downing Street about his ties to Epstein before and during his time as UK ambassador to the US.

But Tory leader Kemi Badenoch said last night: “For so long, the Prime Minister has tried to make this about Peter Mandelson’s lies.

“What this shows is that it is Keir Starmer himself who has lied repeatedly since the very start to the whole country about what he knew, when and how.



“And these files have exposed the fact the PM has not been honest with the country or with the Commons about the extent to which he knew about this relationship.”

Reform UK’s Zia Yusuf piled in, saying: “Starmer was advised and warned about Mandelson’s appalling friendship with Epstein.

“Starmer appointed him anyway. Starmer lied. Starmer must resign.”



A two-page “due diligence” report, drawn up by the Cabinet Office in December 2024 before Mandelson’s appointment, outlined his close ties with Epstein.

The report, titled Advice to the Prime Minister and marked Official Sensitive, noted the relationship continued after Epstein’s 2008 conviction in the US for procuring an underage girl.

It cited a 2019 report by bank JP Morgan that found Epstein appeared to “maintain a particularly close relationship with Prince Andrew the Duke of York and Lord Peter Mandelson”.

Their relationship “continued across 2009-2011, beginning when Lord Mandelson was business minister and continuing after the end of the Labour Government”.

The report also said “Lord Mandelson reportedly stayed in Epstein’s house while he was in jail in June 2009” — but the PM still took the gamble on giving him the job.



Officials warned the PM that the appointment could pose a personal risk to him if it later backfired.

A note read: “If anything goes wrong, you could be more exposed as the individual is more connected to you personally.”

The section of this report concluded: “To note — general reputational risk.”

The papers also revealed senior civil servants had concerns.

Details of a call with Mike Ostheimer, the PM’s general counsel, revealed security adviser Mr Powell “raised concerns about the individual and reputation” to the PM’s then-Chief of Staff Morgan McSweeney.



The note said: “MM (Mr McSweeney) responded that the issues had been addressed” as part of a fact-finding process surrounding his sacking.

Sir Philip Barton, then-Foreign Office permanent under-secretary, “also had reservations around the appointment”, it said.



The documents revealed that Mandelson was offered a top-secret briefing for his new role before he had even passed the highest level of government vetting.

Emails show Foreign Office officials planned to brief him on “higher tiers” of classified information weeks after his appointment was announced.

But records suggest his full security clearance was not confirmed until January 30, more than a month later.

He was then asked to apply for an even more sensitive level of clearance known as STRAP, which was not initiated until February.



Last night, Shadow Cabinet Minister Alex Burghart said: “The lapse in the PM’s judgment knows no bounds.

“Allowing a scandal-ridden former minister access to highly-sensitive information before proper clearance is completely careless.

“Even more troubling is this happened while the Government was aware of Mandelson’s long-standing, close connections to Epstein.”

Officials were told to carry out welfare checks on Mandelson every day “for a while” after his sacking.

And the files show that after he was fired, Mandelson said to the Foreign Office: “My chief concern is leaving the US and arriving in the UK with the maximum dignity and minimum media intrusion which I think is to the advantage of all concerned, not least because I remain a crown/civil servant and expect to be treated as such.



“How is the FCDO assisting in this?”

Yesterday Darren Jones, Chief Secretary to the PM, told MPs the Government “wouldn’t have wanted to pay £1” to Lord Mandelson but it was cheaper than going to an employment tribunal.

He added: “The Prime Minister has since said that Peter Mandelson should either return that money or donate it.”

The due diligence report handed to the PM also highlighted Mandelson’s past role as a non-executive director of a Russian conglomerate which owned a defence company linked to the military.

The document included a media report which said Mandelson remained on its board until June 2017 — long after Vladimir Putin’s annexation of Crimea in 2014.



It also referred to a “flowery account” on the website of his firm, Global Counsel, of his 2018 meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping.

The file highlighted a speech Mandelson gave to the University of Hong Kong. And it said Mandelson is “seen as an advocate for closer UK-China relations”.

Mandelson was arrested at his London home last month and is currently subject to a police investigation over allegations of misconduct in public office. He denies any wrongdoing.

The ambassador row has had major consequences inside the Government, prompting the resignation of Mr McSweeney, who had lobbied for the peer’s appointment.

The files are only the first tranche of tens of thousands of documents due to be released.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

Ministers last night said they will review the National Security Vetting system, including looking at the lessons learned from the scandal.

News.title: Man fighting for life hospital after train crashes into car at level crossing and emergency teams flood scene
News.content:
TRAIN SMASH Man fighting for life hospital after train crashes into car at level crossing and emergency teams flood scene

A MAN is fighting for his life in hospital after a horror collision between a car and a train on a level crossing.

Emergency services scrambled to the scene this afternoon after reports a train had struck the vehicle on the rural crossing at Richborough near Sandwich, Kent.



The man, who has not yet been named, was airlifted to a specialist hospital in London in a life-threatening condition.

Cops have now confirmed they are investigating the collision which took place just before 4.30pm on Tuesday.



No other injuries were reported at the scene.

A British Transport Police spokesperson said: “Officers were called to the line near Sandwich at 4.32pm today following reports that a train had struck a car at a level crossing in Richborough.

“Officers attended, alongside paramedics, and a man was airlifted to hospital with life-threatening injuries.

“There were no other injuries reported, and enquiries are ongoing into the circumstances leading up to the collision.”



Train services were delayed while investigations were carried out at the scene.

News.title: Mum-of-four dies after getting Turkey tummy tuck as cops launch murder probe
News.content:
SURGERY TRAGEDY Mum-of-four dies after getting Turkey tummy tuck as cops launch murder probe

A MOTHER-OF-FOUR has died after travelling to Turkey for surgery as authorities launch a murder probe into her death.

Jessika Chagnon Gailloux, 35, travelled to the coastal city of Antalya for a tummy tuck and breast lift at a private clinic.





Not long after the operation, Jessika’s condition suddenly deteriorated.

Medical staff reportedly worked frantically to save her life, but despite their efforts they could not save her.

Originally from French-speaking Quebec, Canada, Jessika died at around 4pm on March 7, according to NeedToKnow.

Authorities have since opened a murder investigation as they seek to establish exactly what happened.

A friend who had travelled to Turkey with Jessika told investigators the pair had taken drugs three days before arriving in the country.

Officials are now looking into whether that could have played any role in the tragedy.

Jessika’s body has since been taken to the Antalya Forensic Medicine Institution, where specialists will carry out a full autopsy.

Jessika’s best friend, Ericka, has launched a GoFundMe campaign to help support her four children.



In a heartfelt tribute, she wrote: “Today, it is with a broken heart that I create this collection.

“Jessika was an extraordinary woman and a deeply loving mother.

“Her sudden loss leaves a huge void in the lives of all those who loved her, but especially in that of her children.

“Every donation, no matter the amount, is a gesture of love that will help her children during this incredibly difficult time.”

A spokesperson for the Antalya police said: “During the checks by the crime scene investigation teams, it was learned that Gailloux had no cuts, stab wounds, or gunshot wounds on her body other than surgical stitch marks related to the operation.



“As part of the investigation, authorities are also reviewing the victim’s health condition before the operation and her activities in the days leading up to her death.

“Investigators are currently trying to determine whether she had consumed narcotic substances before she died.”

The tragedy comes less than a week after the FCDO issued a warning to anyone considering cosmetic procedures in Turkey.

Large numbers of UK tourists travel to the country each year seeking cheaper surgery, but officials say standards between clinics can vary dramatically.

The warning followed the deaths of seven British nationals linked to cosmetic procedures in Turkey last year.



In November, a British tourist collapsed after having a hair transplant followed by dental treatment during a short trip to Istanbul.

Turkish outlet Istanbul Today reported say Mentor Rama, 36, returned to his hotel and fell suddenly ill, prompting staff to alert emergency services.

He was rushed by ambulance to a nearby hospital, where he was pronounced dead at around 4.30pm.

His body was promptly sent to the Council of Forensic Medicine for an autopsy.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Officials have not yet released a cause of death.



After the examination, his remains were repatriated to the UK.

News.title: BA pilot arrested for ‘secretly filming sex sessions with 16 women’ as shocked stewardess reveals how he was caught
News.content:
A BRITISH Airways pilot has been arrested over claims he secretly filmed sex sessions with up to 16 women — and shared the footage online without their consent.

The allegations came after his partners and ex-partners realised footage of their encounters was being shared on sleazy websites.





The Sun understands that stewardesses warned each other of the pilot and his alleged plan to covertly film romps.

He is believed to have wooed women from other airlines during hotel stays between flights across the globe.

Some of the alleged victims reported the pilot to the Met Police, which confirmed it had launched an investigation.

The allegations reportedly involve a group of women aged 24 to 36.

One woman, who alleges she was targeted between 2023 and 2025, told a friend: “I was being recorded during sexual encounters without my consent.

“I didn’t realise until another woman in the same position mentioned it to me.

“He would position his laptop with music on, but screen off, in front of us during sexual encounters.



“Now, footage has gone out online and it has emerged he was doing this to 15 other women.”

Another woman allegedly discovered multiple videos on his laptop. It is claimed he taped over a light which indicated the computer’s camera was on.

A source said: “She saw different girls, different rooms, different everything. She saw people wearing different uniforms.

“She saw women in uniforms from Virgin Atlantic, so he’s targeting other women from different airlines.”

Industry bosses also received reports of alleged cocaine use by the pilot.



The Met said: “Officers arrested a 31-year-old man on Thursday, 5 March on suspicion of voyeurism.

“The arrest followed an allegation made to police on Tuesday, March 3, of videos being taken and distributed without consent. The man has been released on bail as enquiries continue.”

A UK Civil Aviation Authority spokesman told The Sun: “We can’t comment on individual cases but reports of substance misuse are being reviewed and any appropriate action will be taken.”

Sources within BA said bosses had stood down the pilot, offering well-being support but banning him from flying until the police investigation concludes.

The airline has not commented.

News.title: Sick woman who used dating apps to meet 10 men before falsely crying rape jailed
News.content:
RAPE LIAR Sick woman who used dating apps to meet 10 men before falsely crying rape jailed

A WICKED woman who falsely accused 10 men of raping her has been jailed for four and a half years.

Stacey Sharples, 31, trawled dating sites to meet men, often going back to their homes then calling the cops and incorrectly alleging she had been raped.



The sick woman pleaded guilty to 10 counts of perverting the course of justice at Bolton Crown Court on Monday.



During her trial, the court heard how Sharples would search dating sites like Plenty of Fish, Badoo and Facebook Dating looking for victims.

After she left their homes, she would often call the police and report she had been raped, even in some cases where no sexual activity had taken place at all.

Sharples, of Farnworth, Bolton claimed she was abused by nine men behind a library in Failsworth, Greater Manchester on Boxing Day 2013.

She made three more false claims in 2017, four in 2018 and 11 in 2019.

She denied 19 charges of perverting justice in April 2024 but admitted ten counts last month at Bolton crown court.

Nine offences were left on file.

Judge Nicholas Clarke told her last week: “Every one of these men are entirely innocent of any criminal behaviour.

“Their lives have been blighted by the allegations. It was all a wicked pack of lies.”



He also branded Sharples a “sexual predator” for her allegations which devastated many victims’ lives.

One victim told The Sun he met Sharples in 2019 on Badoo, where her profile had a topless picture.

He said: “Her messages said she was after one thing. She was in my house for about 20 minutes.

“We kissed then she said she didn’t want to kiss any more. I said no problem and got her a taxi home.

“Then she phoned and said I really hurt her.”



Sharples rang police and claimed the 40-year-old builder raped and attacked her.

Twelve cops arrested him at his Salford flat, he spent two days in cells and lost his job.

He was later told he faced no further action.

Nine of the men falsely accused by Sharples provided impact statements to the court detailing the impact of the allegations, including family breakdowns and losing their jobs.



Reece Lockett’s statement said: “My life was turned upside down the day I was falsely accused of rape.

“My mum and dad disowned me. My partner of five years left me. I lost my part-time job.”

Detective Sergeant Steven Gilliland of Greater Manchester Police, who investigated the case, said in the course of its investigation, “the evidence continued to demonstrate that the reports were untrue”.

He also paid tribute to the courage of the men who were victims of her false accusations, “who have endured an experience no one would ever wish to go through”.

Gilliland added: “We took the allegations made by Stacey Sharples seriously, explored all lines of enquiry and swiftly made arrests or interviewed all the men she accused.



“We gave her multiple opportunities to provide further explanation or information to us, after interviews with the men and subsequent evidence uncovered didn’t align with her first recollection, as we understand that trauma can impact how victims and survivors recount their experiences.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“Ultimately, as the evidence continued to demonstrate that the reports were untrue, coupled with the desire for justice from some of the men who had been falsely accused, it was right that we followed the evidence and pursued the individual who had actually committed a criminal offence.

“I would like to pay tribute to the strength of these men, who have endured an experience no one would ever wish to go through, and done so with dignity. I hope they feel a sense of justice for what happened to them today.”

News.title: Moment lawyer’s wife tussles with neighbour over privacy fence before saying he’s ‘old man’ in danger of ‘heart attack’
News.content:
THIS is the moment a lawyer’s wife tussles with a neighbour over a privacy fence.

Julia Stafford, 45, was filmed lashing out at the neighbour outside her £850,000 home.





Robin Christie, 65, had attempted to stop plans to erect a security fence when the episode unfolded, reports MailOnline.

Ms Stafford and her husband Samuel Tempest Brooks, 44, were granted planning permission for an extension to their posh home in July 2024.

But their plans to knock down a boundary wall and replace it with a gated entrance were not given approval.



The couple decided to demolish the wall regardless leaving neighbouring residents fuming in the posh area of Richmond-upon-Thames in London.

In shocking footage, business consultant Christie can be seen grasping a set of metal railings in an attempt to stop the couple’s construction work going ahead.

Ms Stafford can be seen trying to kick out at Christie before the railings come crashing down before she starts screaming at the woman filming.

She can be heard shouting: “That’s what I said would happen if I let go. You stupid woman!”



Ms Stafford is also heard hurling insults at Christie, including calling him at “old man” who is in danger of a “heart attack”.

The case was heard at Wimbledon Magistrates’ Court where the judge said Christie had gone too far in objecting to the illegal work.

The business consultant was convicted of harassing Ms Stafford and could face up to 26 weeks in prison when he is sentenced later this month.

The filmed dispute occurred over security fencing erected on the residents’ side of the brick wall during construction.

A group of locals had expressed their opposition to the work- which they argued would bring loud HGVs in and out of the peaceful cul-de-sac.



Ms Stafford told the court how Christie and another neighbour “began objecting to me erecting fencing and they were also verbally abusive”.

“I felt very threatened, violated and exposed and I kept being accused of criminal damage,” she added.

Judge Evans said of Christie’s behaviour in the video: “He can be seen pushing against the complainant and in the scuffle her hands and legs were hurt.

“Getting into a tussle over fencing, Mr Christie was completely losing perspective and behaving in a high–handed manner.”

Ms Stafford claimed the feud, which was conducted over months of retaliations, caused her PTSD.



The group of furious neighbours engaged in an escalating battle with the homeowners, with both parties fixing various bike locks to the metal fence.

Christie eventually took and axle grinder and bolt cutters to remove the locks from the fence.

Judge Evans said: “Placing the bike and multiple locks is an act of harassment.

“Putting a lock on someone’s property and then watching them so he and the community can get their own way.”

Christie said the judge’s remarks were “ferocious”, but argued “she was only allowed to hear half of the story”.



In a witness statement for the defence, council leader Gareth Roberts confirmed he had told Ms Stafford she would face planning enforcement and even prosecution if she persisted in demolishing the wall.

In court, Christie argued: “The wall was in the conservation area and she had no permission to demolish this wall.

“She was agitated and angry and would stress her opinion that she was correct.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“It would have been a breach of planning if she demolished the wall.”

The major construction work on the Richmond Upon Thames home did stop, though, and the house has been listed for sale for just shy of £1million.

News.title: Moment armed police swarm Thorpe St Andrew's school after teen girl 'stabbed'
News.content:
EXCLUSIVE: Moment armed police swarm Thorpe St Andrew's school after teen girl 'stabbed'

Video footage captured from inside a classroom showed the moment armed police gathered outside a classroom after a teen girl was 'stabbed' as Thorpe St Andrew's school in Norwich

Footage shows panic from inside classroom at Thorpe St Andrew school

Video footage has captured the moment armed police swarmed outside a classroom after a teenage girl was 'stabbed' at school.

Terrified pupils hid under their desks and texted their parents begging to go home as the drama unfolded on Wednesday morning. In a new video, obtained exclusively by the Mirror, alarmed pupils can be heard asking "What's happening', "What is happening?" as they spot firearms cops moving together outside a classroom window.

Schoolchildren were ordered to hide and turn off their phones while teachers barricaded classroom doors after reports that the girl had been injured. A 16-year-old boy was later arrested just before 10.30am.

View 4 Images A 16-year-old boy is being quizzed on suspicion of causing grievous bodily harm with intent (Image: X)

Officers raced to the scene at Thorpe St Andrew School, Norwich, as around 100 parents gathered after hearing about the incident on social media. One parent was heard shouting: "Just f*****g let the kids out." Another said: "If my son is under a table and someone’s coming with a knife, I want him to be aware they’re coming."

One pupil who made contact with their family sent a text reading "I am very scared", while another sent a message saying they "just want to go home". Police drones were also on the scene as officers searched for the suspect, who is thought to have fled after jumping over a fence, before an arrest was made.

View 4 Images A teenage girl suffered minor injuries after she was stabbed at Thorpe St Andrew School near Norwich (Image: Newsquest / SWNS)

It's understood that the suspect was on the run for more than an hour, with the arrest being made at around 11.30am. Officers said a 15-year-old boy is being quizzed on suspicion of causing grievous bodily harm with intent. The teenage girl, understood to be a pupil at the school, was taken to the hospital with "minor injuries".

A teacher at the school said they had been told to stay in the classroom with pupils, telling ITV: "One of the things you practice, but never think you'll do." Parent, Adam Howlett, said earlier that he had still not heard from his daughter, despite his son saying he is still locked in his classroom.

View 4 Images Police said the teenage suspect had been taken to Wymondham Police Investigation Centre for questioning (Image: Newsquest / SWNS)

He said: "When it comes to your child even minor injuries are terrifying. You don’t expect to come to school and get injured. I’m just waiting to find out some news and put my mind at rest." Another parent, Shantelle Taylor, said she was "absolutely fuming" to hear about the incident through a friend and not through the school.

The school, which was rated Good in its 2025 Ofsted inspection, is an 11-to-18 mixed secondary school which also includes a sixth form. A Norfolk Police spokesperson said: "A boy has been arrested following an incident in Thorpe St Andrew. Police were called to a school on Laundry Lane, Thorpe St Andrew at 10.24am this morning following reports that a teenage girl had been stabbed. Emergency services attended the scene including fire and ambulance. The victim has been taken to hospital with minor injuries.

View 4 Images Armed police officers raced to the school on Wednesday morning (Image: Newsquest / SWNS)

"A 15-year-old boy has been arrested on suspicion of causing grievous bodily harm with intent. He has been taken to Wymondham Police Investigation Centre for questioning. This incident is not believed to be linked to a hate crime reported at the same school last week."

A statement from Broad Horizons Education Trust, which runs Thorpe St Andrew School, said: "We have clear procedures in place and our staff acted swiftly, with the whole school going into lockdown with children staying in their classroom with their teacher.

Article continues below

"One of our students was injured and is now being treated in hospital for minor injuries. This has obviously been a distressing experience for our whole community and our focus now is on making sure all students feel supported as we begin to understand what happened."

News.title: Donald Trump's unhinged 24 hours as he says a very sweaty Jake Paul should run for office
News.content:
Donald Trump's unhinged 24 hours as he says a very sweaty Jake Paul should run for office

Formerly popular YouTuber-turned bang average boxer and Trump supporter Jake Paul was at what became an increasingly surreal rally in Kentucky

View Image Wherever Jake Paul was standing must have been extremely warm

Donald Trump's "affordability tour" took a surreal turn tonight in Kentucky, as the US President whooped up his fans to cheer for the Iran war that's sending their gas prices through the roof.

‌



The US President spent far more time wanging on about the war, Gavin Newsom and his feud with Thomas Massie, a Kentucky Republican House member who pressed for the release of the Epstein files than he did the cost of living.

‌



Formerly popular YouTuber turned bang average boxer and Trump supporter Jake Paul was at the speech, and got up on stage.

‌



And then someone passed out in the crowd and Dr Oz helped her.

I wish I was making this up.

Meanwhile in Trumpworld

‌



Trump revealed the one thing he admires about Obama

Someone fainted, Trump played a song, Dr Oz helped out

Jake Paul was extremely sweaty

Here's what you need to know.

1. Trump declares "we've won" Iran war

Trump did his very best Team America impression, almost singing "OPERATION EPIC FURY! Is that a great name? Well, it's only good if you win, and we've won."

‌



He continued: "Let me tell you. We've won. You know, you never liked to say too early you won. We won."

It's unclear by what measure he declared victory, and there is no indication any change in American deployment is imminent.

But he sure did bang on about how AWESOME the operation's name was.

‌



"They gave me a list of names. 'Sir, you can pick the name you'd like, sir.' I said, the name of what? 'The name of the attack! On Iran, sir.' And they gave me like 20 names. And I'm like falling asleep. I didn't like any of them. Then I see Epic Fury."

2. The speech was interrupted by a medical emergency

Donald Trump's rally speech in Kentucky was interrupted tonight, as a medical emergency unfolded immediately behind the US President.

An elderly Trump supporter standing on the podium behind Trump began to sway and looked unwell. Other supporters moved out of the way to allow her to brace herself on the barrier - but after a few moments she appeared to lose consciousness and was helped to the floor.

‌



Oblivious to what was unfolding behind him, Trump continued his speech - in which he was at the time attacking California Governor Gavin Newsom - before being alerted to the situation by people shouting "medic, medic."

Trump turned and saw what was happening and said: "Is there a doctor in the house please?" He told those rushing to the fan's aide to "take your time....we have all the time. These people are great people. We have all the time."

Content cannot be displayed without consent

‌



3. While the speech was paused, Trump asked for Ave Maria to be played. Then Dr Oz turned up

As medics arrived to assist, Trump added: "First responders are incredible, by the way." He then said to the crowd: "Wanna hear a song? Let's see if the people backstage are listening to me. How about "Ave Maria?"

TV doctor-turned Trump administration health official Dr Mehmet Oz was among those assisting the woman who collapsed.

"It's Dr Oz, can you believe it?" Trump said as the crowd applauded. "Dr Oz! Wow, he's a good doctor."

‌



Content cannot be displayed without consent

4. He revealed the one thing about Obama that he admires

Trump revealed that the one thing he admired about Barack Obama "which was nothing really", was the way he would "bob" down the stairs of Air Force One.

Trump even did a little impersonation of his predecessor bobbing down the stairs.

‌



"You talk about unpresendential", he said of a man physically able to walk down stairs without clinging on for dear life.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

5. Jake Paul got up on stage and nobody really seemed to know who he was

Jake Paul, who started out as an edgy YouTuber and somehow became a well-paid but average boxer, was also there.

‌



He got up on stage looking very sweaty. Either nobody knew who he was, or everyone in Kentucky hates him because the applause for him was pretty muted.

Trump promised to endorse Paul if he ever ran for political office. Because the year is 2026 and we exist in the worst timeline.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

‌



6. He had a lengthy pop at one of his own congressmen - who voted to release the Epstein files

Trump launched into a lengthy rant about Thomas Massie, a Republican member of the House of Representatives for Kentucky.

"We have to get rid of this loser," Trump said. "He is disloyal to the Republican party, disloyal to the people of Kentucky and most importantly, disloyal to the United States of America"

Massie's crime? He added his name to the petition forcing the release of the Epstein files.

‌



7. Trump threatens Iran's new Supreme Leader

Ahead of the rally, Trump issued a thinly-veiled threat against Iran's new Supreme Leader - telling reporters: "We'll see what happens to them".

He dismissed the war as a "little excursion" for the US, which had been "easier" than anticipated.

And he played down skyrocketing fuel costs caused by the war, claiming oil prices would be "coming down" soon.

‌



Iran this week named Mojtaba Khamenei as its new Supreme Leader after a US air strike killed his father, the late Ayatollah Ali Khameni.

Asked earlier whether he thought the US could declare a victory in the war with Khamenei remaining as Supreme Leader, Trump was unusually coy, saying he didn't want to comment.

But later, during a factory tour in Cincinnati, Ohio, Trump issued a further threat to Iran's leaders.

‌



He said that the US had "knocked out twice their leadership" in Iran, and added: "Now they have a new group coming up. Let's see what happens to them."

"We did a little excursion," Trump said. "We had to take a couple of weeks, a few weeks of excursion, but it's been incredible, the military, the job they're doing is incredible.

"I would say, to put it mildly, way ahead of schedule."

‌



He went on: "We've knocked out their Navy, their military in all forms. We've knocked out just about everything there is, including their leadership, twice."

8. Mandelson touted US tech deal as his 'personal pride and joy'

After being fired as our man in Washington last September, Peter Mandelson wrote a farewell email to British Embassy staff - and described the then-forthcoming tech deal between the UK and US as his "personal pride and joy which will help write the next chapter of the special relationship."

A memorandum of understand on Tech Prosperity Deal deal, which was inked at Chequers during Trump's State Visit to the UK the following week, set out the UK and US' plans to "collaborate closely in the build-out of powerful AI infrastructure" and "promot[e] U.S. and UK AI exports to offer the full stack of chips, data centres, and models".

‌



Within a couple of months the deal was iced by the US as Trump tried to pressure the UK to give more ground on wider trade talks. But it was reportedly revived late last month.

God only knows what its status is now, given the breakdown in relations between Trump and Starmer.

But the tech justice non-profit Foxglove wants to know more about Mandelson's involvement - especially given the links between his lobbying firm Global Counsel - in which he still held a stake until February this year - and AI tech giants like Palantir and OpenAI.

‌



Donald Campbell, advocacy director at tech justice non-profit Foxglove said: "These documents raise yet more questions over the UK Government's deals with Trump and his Big Tech allies - and the role played by Peter Mandelson in the agreement announced during Trump's state visit last year.

"If Lord Mandelson, as his email suggests, played a key role in negotiating the UK-US tech deal, while continuing to hold a stake in a lobbying firm representing Palantir and OpenAI, it raises serious concerns over conflicts of interest.

"Yet, looking at the due diligence documents from the time of his appointment, references to either Palantir or OpenAI are strikingly absent.

Article continues below

"The UK Government must urgently set out what role the former Ambassador played in this deal, and how these conflicts were managed. Otherwise it will only further reinforce the impression that it is far too happy to have its priorities set by President Trump and his Big Tech billionaire cronies."

News.title: Radio DJ among six killed in Switzerland bus fire after man 'set himself alight'
News.content:
Radio DJ among six killed in Switzerland bus fire after man 'set himself alight'

A young radio DJ is among six people killed in a devastating bus fire which also left five people injured.

Lara Baumgartner, 26, died after the blaze erupted and gutted the entire vehicle in Kerzers, Fribourg, Switzerland, last night. Local police later confirmed six people had died and at least five were injured after flames reportedly travelled several meters high, with a massive plume of smoke visible for miles from the scene.

Swiss police have launched a probe into the incident following reports that a man had set himself on fire.

In a tribute to Lara, her employer, Radio Energy, wrote: "We are shocked and deeply saddened. In these difficult hours, our thoughts are with Lara's family and loved ones."

Image: /X) /X)

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

Lara's colleagues described her as "a wonderful person and an exceptional colleague".

The message continued: "Her laughter, her warmth, her subtle humour, and her intelligent, loving nature touched many people. She leaves a void that is almost impossible to put into words."

Freiburg police said a 16-year-old boy and a 63-year-old Portuguese bus driver were among the six killed. A 29-year-old man, and two women aged 25 and 29, also tragically died. All victims were Swiss nationals who lived in the area.

Image: Etat de Fribourg - Staat Freibur) Etat de Fribourg - Staat Freibur)

According to police, the suspected perpetrator - a 65-year-old Swiss citizen who lived in Bern - is among the dead. He had been reported missing by his family hours before the fire, which broke out at around 6.25pm on Tuesday. The bus was travelling a route from Düdingen to Kerzers when the fire erupted just before the final stop.

Officers identified those who were injured as two Swiss men aged 34 and 61, two Swiss women aged 27 and 56, and a 32-year-old man from Kosovo. Three of the five injured people were taken to the hospital, while the other two were treated at the scene.

Yesterday, police said the fire may have been sparked by an "arsonist act" and explained they had received information that a person had doused themselves with gasoline. "We have information that a person was responsible for the fire," officers said.

News.title: Donald Trump rally speech interrupted by medical emergency - 'we need a medic!'
News.content:
Donald Trump rally speech interrupted by medical emergency - 'we need a medic!'

Oblivious to what was unfolding, Trump continued his speech - in which he was at the time attacking California Governor Gavin Newsom - before being alerted to the situation by people shouting "medic, medic"

View Image Trump supporters cried out for a medic, interrupting Trump's speech

Donald Trump's rally speech in Kentucky was interrupted tonight, as a medical emergency unfolded immediately behind the US President.

‌



An elderly Trump supporter standing on the podium behind Trump began to sway and looked unwell. Other supporters moved out of the way to allow her to brace herself on the barrier - but after a few moments she appeared to lose consciousness and was helped to the floor.

‌



Oblivious to what was unfolding behind him, Trump continued his speech - in which he was at the time attacking California Governor Gavin Newsom - before being alerted to the situation by people shouting "medic, medic."

‌



Trump turned and saw what was happening and said: "Is there a doctor in the house please?" He told those rushing to the fan's aide to "take your time....we have all the time. These people are great people. We have all the time."

Content cannot be displayed without consent

As medics arrived to assist, Trump added: "First responders are incredible, by the way." He then said to the crowd: "Wanna hear a song? Let's see if the people backstage are listening to me. How about "Ave Maria?"

‌



TV doctor-turned Trump administration health official Dr Mehmet Oz was among those assisting the woman who collapsed.

"It's Dr Oz, can you believe it?" Trump said as the crowd applauded. "Dr Oz! Wow, he's a good doctor."

Content cannot be displayed without consent

‌



Trump is visiting Kentucky on his "affordability tour". In November, the White House announced that Trump would travel the country more frequently in an attempt to stave off a drubbing in this year's midterm elections by showing he’s taking America's cost of living crisis seriously.

Up to the point of interruption, he had spent most of the speech talking about the war in Iran, which has sent US petrol prices skyrocketing.

The US national average petrol price as of today is $3.578 a gallon - nearly 60 cents higher than it was a month ago, according to the US motoring and travel organisation AAA.

Article continues below

He also gave several shout-outs to Youtuber-turned mid-level boxer Jake Paul, who is in the audience. Paul is a Trump supporter.

News.title: Trump claims Iran war will end 'soon' because there is 'nothing left to target'
News.content:
Trump claims Iran war will end 'soon' because there is 'nothing left to target'

Donald Trump has offered shifting war goals since he and his Israeli counterpart Benjamin Netanyahu ordered strikes on the Iranian capital Tehran earlier this month

View 3 Images Donald Trump said he expected the war in Iran to end "soon" (Image: Getty Images)

Donald Trump has claimed the war in Iran will end "soon" as there is apparently "practically nothing left to target."

‌



The US President appears to have signalled an off ramp to exit its intervention in Iran after it unleashed air strikes alongside Israel earlier this month. Trump said "the war is going great." He added: "We are way ahead of the timetable. We have done more damage than we thought possible, even in the original six-week period."

‌



Trump later continued: "Any time I want it to end, it will end." The claim will add to the mixed messaging Trump has made of the longevity of the war.

‌



View 3 Images Trump has offered different war goals following his intervention in Iran (Image: Getty Images)

The comments Trump made to outlet Axios indicates Trump is keen to tell the American people that the country will not get bogged down in another lengthy war in the Middle East. Trump previously said of Iran: “They are paying for 47 years of death and destruction they caused. This is payback. They will not get off that easy."

The Trump administration has offered multiple war aims including stopping Iran from obtaining a nuclear weapon, protecting the US and Europe from an imminent threat and regime change. Trump's shifting justifications for the war have been met with scepticism among a significant proportion of his base, many of whom voted Republican in 2024 in part over his promise not to drag the US into new wars.

‌



Trump marched into the war without building a case for it and is now dealing with rising oil prices as suppliers flag concerns about supplies coming through the Strait of Hormuz. Trump today said the fluctuations in oil prices a "matter of war."

View 3 Images Israel and the US have launched strikes against Iran and its proxies (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

The US President has also wavered over whether to call the military operation a war. When speaking to a journalist about whether it was a war or an excursion, Trump said: "Well, it's both."

Article continues below

"It's an excursion that will keep us out of a war...for them it's a war, for us it's turned out to be easier than we thought."

The US has claimed Iran's missile attacks have been reduced by some 90 per cent since the tart of the war nearly 11 days ago. It is believed Iran may have had as many as 100,000 deadly drones at the beginning of the war, 5,000 short range missiles and a possible 2,500 ballistic missiles.

Sixty per cent of its launchers have been hit by air-strikes and it is believed 2,410 ballistic missiles remain, whilst Iran has up to 200 missile launchers left.

News.title: Horror lorry crash sees tyres fly '30ft in air' as vehicle erupts into flames
News.content:
A horror two vehicle crash saw a lorry burst into flames on a major road, causing tyres to fly "30 feet into air".

Emergency services raced to A1 between the Thistly Cross roundabout and Haddington in East Lothian, Scotland, at around 3.50pm this afternoon. The road was immediately sealed off with drivers urged to avoid the area. Shocking footage taken at the crash site captured a lorry covered in flames, with thick plumes of smoke pouring out of the vehicle, Edinburgh Live reports.

A witness at the scene, Mark Innes, said: "It carried forward with the momentum, driving along the motorway and it probably almost instantly burst into flames. It was terrifying. It was honestly like something out of a film. It's good that everyone is okay.

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

"I got my van into a safe position, and by that point that was a few bystanders and other drivers helping. By the time I ran over the driver was out, he was obviously very shaken and panicked. The lorry was exploding. There were tyres blowing thirty feet into the air and crashing down, going into the nearby bushes and setting them on fire."

Police Scotland said: "The A1 is closed northbound between the Thistly Cross roundabout and the Haddington turn off following a two-vehicle crash reported to police around 3.50pm on Wednesday, 11 March. Road users are advised to avoid the area."

Traffic Scotland earlier told drivers to "expect delays" and "use alternative routes" as the A1 was closed northbound at Haddington at around 4pm.

News.title: Moment Magaluf rape gang's teen Brit victim rescued - and 'wolf pack' caught
News.content:
Moment Magaluf rape gang's teen Brit victim rescued - and 'wolf pack' caught

The 18-year-old victim was attacked by a group of men - known as the "wolf pack" - who were sentenced to a combined 73 years after they took a plea bargain to avoid more than 150 years if convicted

View 5 Images The eight men have been sentenced to multiple years in prison (Image: SOLARPIX.COM/J.Bastida)

A hotel security guard seized the moment to rescue a British teenage gang-rape victim with a group, dubbed the "wolf pack," being arrested over the horror sex attack.

The seven French men and one Swiss national, known as the "wolf pack" accepted prison sentences totalling just over 73 years after they struck a plea bargain deal with prosecutors just days before their trial over the rape was due to begin. Before deciding to plead guilty, they had been warned they faced being jailed for more than 150 years if convicted.

They were arrested after a British 18-year-old was gang-raped at the BH Mallorca Resort in Magaluf. The thugs recorded the rape with some in the group whipping and slapping her as others chanted sick encouragement. The footage recorded by the men proved key in their arrests and the prosecution that followed.

View 5 Images The eight men took a plea deal after the woman was gang raped (Image: SOLARPIX.COM)

Sickening reality of party town Magaluf from gang rape to 'manosphere' terror READ MORE :

A security guard at the hotel where the incident happened came to the rescue of the British teen and found her sobbing on the floor in the lobby. He had just arrived for his morning shift and described the police response at the time was said to have been very quick and efficient.

Bruises were found on the victim's arm during a medical examination and are believed to have been caused when she was held down. She was also found to have suffered a cut to her chest.

The eight men who were convicted have since been named and have been ordered to compensate their victim with more than €100,000 (£86,313). French tourists Khalil Abderrahmen Mejbri and Romain Charles Michel Galatioto raped their victim, who had been drinking heavily and "fell into a state of unconscious," the court heard.

The sickening footage recorded by the men, who came from different groups and didn’t all know each other before they targeted their victim, proved key in their arrests and subsequent successful prosecution

French national Anthony Guy Gerard Wengler and Swiss national Haiaiel Ngandjeu Sjamen Tchatchou raped her at "least once," as well as Thomas Eric Coretin Wisniewski who is also from France. Tchatchou and Wengler spat at the victim and and whipped her along with Mejbri after Frenchman Satilmis Sahan slapped her.

View 5 Images The men have been ordered to compensate the victim (Image: SOLARPIX.COM)

French national Anthony Auque, who was holidaying with Wengler, and fellow Frenchman Mouhamadou Wade watched on while the 30-minute gang rape occurred, surrounding the victim along with the rest of the group chanting sick encouragement during the attack.

The plea agreement all eight men accepted and signed, says: “Each one of the defendants with their mobile phones, during the incidents previously described, recorded several videos.

"Anthony Auque recorded with his mobile phone 14 videos lasting 170 seconds in total; Khalil Abderrahmen Mejbri recorded with his mobile phone five videos lasting 142 seconds; Mohamadou Wade recorded with his mobile phone two videos lasting a total of 26 seconds and took a photograph; and Anthony Guy Gerard Wengler; Satilmis Sahan; Romain Charles Michel Galatioto; Thomas Eric Coretin Wisniewski; and Lucas Haiaiel Ngandjeu Djamen Tchatchou did the same on at least one occasion.

View 5 Images The rape happened at a Magaluf hotel (Image: SOLARPIX.COM)

"Boasting about the actions taken and with the clear intention of humiliating and violating his victim, Mejbri and Wade sent and shared the content of their recordings through the Snapchat app.

"Those recordings were taken during the time in which their victim was semi-conscious or unconscious so that she was unable to consent to it." Mejbri and Wisniewski accepted a nine-year prison sentence for a crime of “sexual assault with carnal access,” a severe form of sexual crime involving non-consensual sexual penetration of the victim.

Wengler, Tchatchou and Galatioto were handed 11-year jail sentences after admitting the same offence. Sahan admitted a crime of “sexual assault without penetration” for which he was given a four-year prison sentence.

View 5 Images The hotel security found the victim in the lobby (Image: SOLARPIX.COM/J.Bastida)

All eight men, the five self-confessed sexual offenders as well as Auque and Wade who admitted to filming their victim being targeted, were each handed two year three month prison sentences after admitting a crime of violation of privacy relating to the recordings they made on their mobile phones.

Sahan, Auque and Wade had already been granted bail before the scheduled start of yesterday’s trial and remain on bail. At least two of them are unlikely to be hauled back to prison because of time they served on remand behind bars before they were granted bail around nine months ago.

The other five remain in prison pending a further court hearing next month where a judge is set to be asked by their defence lawyers to agree to kick them out of Spain once they have served part of their jail time.

Article continues below
